finding: atomic swap preserves all side effects; finalizeTailWindow collapses synthetic-id and real-id cases to one row without dropping older pages

switched-away branch runs set(clearStreaming) unconditionally, writing global streaming flags onto whatever conversation is now open — a background-streaming conversation B switched-into during the getHistory(A) await gets streamingMessage nulled and its content frames dropped

streaming flags held true across await getHistory; switching away from A mid-await snapshots A as isStreaming:true (mid-finalize); returning restores it perpetually generating and reloadOpen bails on state.isStreaming so it cannot self-heal short of a full reload
switched-away branch writes lastTurnInterrupted (A's cancelled) onto the now-open conversation; original wrote it before any await while on-screen
error-frame handler + sendMessage catch reset isStreaming/streamingMessage without resetting finalizingTurn; masked by frame serialization but violates the reset-everywhere invariant
isStreaming (generating affordance) cleared one afterStreamComplete+getHistory RTT later than the original synchronous clear; held up during a hung fetch
finalizeTailWindow copies the window Map twice (base.delete then appendWindow's copy) — one redundant O(window) copy per turn, bounded, negligible
the toHaveCount(0) notice assertion is not load-bearing in the text-answer scenario (isStreaming shadows finalizing); finalizing empty-turn suppression is unit-covered (TEST-2)
finalizing threaded identically to isStreaming/interrupted in both render paths; all PLAN items implemented + covered

NEW-1: afterStreamComplete awaited outside try could strand finalizingTurn on an extension throw — REJECTED: registry.afterStreamComplete (registry.tsx:681-699) wraps every extension+actions in try/catch and never throws

NEW-2: finalizingTurn single global flag — double-finalize across a switch could clear another conversation's suppression; FIXED: switched-away branches no longer write finalizingTurn (switch/reset already reset it)

NEW-3: switch-away mid-finalize then cache-hit return could flash the notice for a reasoning-only-streamed-but-persisted-has-text turn — REJECTED (non-actionable residual): NOT a regression (base showed a MISSING assistant turn, strictly worse), self-heals on next sync, requires an unusual streamed-reasoning-only-yet-persisted-has-text condition; a robust fix (snapshotting the persisted tail on switch-away) is disproportionate to a self-healing cosmetic low
getHistory(id,limit=MESSAGE_PAGE_SIZE) + PaginatedMessages fields (messages/has_more_before/has_more_after) consumed exactly as the unchanged reconcileTail; snap-to-tail vs merge chosen correctly

finalizing threaded identically to isStreaming/interrupted in both the virtualized and plain render paths; ChatMessage's new prop mirrors interrupted's shape/default/JSDoc; emptyCompletion adds !finalizing alongside !interrupted

finalizeTailWindow follows the messageWindow.ts helper idiom (pure, Map-in/out, doc-comment, node:test); the e2e follows repo conventions (fixtures/test-context, byTestId, sse-mock-helpers); no hand-rolled duplication
every PLAN ITEM (1-7) implemented with file:line evidence and mapped to a TEST; no item claimed-but-absent; ITEM-6 correctly ships as verify-only (existing #135 spec) per DEC-4

ITER2: reuse-then-append correct vs backend contract — streaming.rs resumes the SAME assistant_message_id, retains existing content, emits only new continuation deltas; sequence_order continues densely; no duplicate text
ITER2: no duplicate tool_use on resume — the MCP extension's existsInStreaming dedup now matches the adopted row
ITER2: genuinely-new turn byte-identical (no existing row for a fresh uuid → fresh empty placeholder); branch/edit/regenerate mint new uuids so no stale adoption

ITER2: reuse path aliases streamingMessage to the persisted row object; safe today (all writers copy-on-write) but the invariant was undocumented — FIXED by documenting the copy-on-write requirement in the helper docstring

ITER2: on resume the first text_delta bypasses provideStreamingContent (adopted row already has a text block) — benign/equivalent block, and finalizeTailWindow replaces the row with DB truth at complete

ITER2: PRE-EXISTING gap (not a regression) — a resume opening with a brand-new tool_use spawns a synthetic second row until finalize; the fixed symptom (row blanked → bubble vanishes) does NOT recur there
